name: Pyro (Probabilistic Programming for PyTorch)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Uber AI / Dustin Tran

performance.notes: PyTorch backend; GPU support; SVI much faster than MCMC

agent_readiness.notes: Universal probabilistic programming for PyTorch. import pyro. import pyro.distributions as dist. def model(data): mu = pyro.sample('mu', dist.Normal(0, 1)); sigma = pyro.sample('sigma', dist.LogNormal(0, 1)); return pyro.sample('obs', dist.Normal(mu, sigma), obs=data). def guide(data): mu_loc = pyro.param('mu_loc', torch.tensor(0.0)); .... SVI: from pyro.infer import SVI, Trace_ELBO. MCMC: from pyro.infer import MCMC, NUTS. Autoguide: pyro.infer.autoguide.AutoNormal. Effect handlers. Vectorized: pyro.plate.

gotchas: Steep learning curve — variational inference concepts required
Guide definition is manual — use autoguides for simpler models
PyTorch knowledge required for custom distributions/likelihoods
NumPyro (JAX version) is faster for many use cases
best_when: Variational inference (SVI + ELBO) with PyTorch models
When PyTorch integration is required for neural-probabilistic models
Research requiring effect handler-based PPL flexibility
avoid_when: Simple Bayesian models (use PyMC for friendlier API)
Production Bayesian inference without SVI knowledge
summary: Pyro is a probabilistic programming language for PyTorch. pyro.sample('mu', dist.Normal(0,1)). pyro.plate for vectorized. SVI with ELBO for variational inference. MCMC/NUTS. Autoguides for automatic VI. Effect handlers for PPL flexibility. Apache-2.0 by Uber AI. Best for deep generative models (VAE, flows) and neural-probabilistic PyTorch models. NumPyro (JAX) is often faster for pure Bayesian inference.
